zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger Childe Baron de Torpel於1055年出生在法國利姆辛可斯勒 普易時，他的父親Alwin Childegaire30歲，母親Lady Alwin Child29歲。他於1089年與Baroness Roger de Torpel結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1130年在英國諾薩普頓史爾享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾比特爾波洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger Childe Baron de Torpel于1055年出生在法国利姆辛可斯勒 普易时，他的父亲Alwin Childegaire30岁，母亲Lady Alwin Child29岁。他于1089年与Baroness Roger de Torpel结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1130年在英国諾薩普頓史爾享年75岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾比特爾波洛。

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Torpel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans Peter, Kurt, Ulrich.
German:
nickname from Middle High German top(p)el ‘game of dice’.

variants.name: Topel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Fritz, Gerd, Heinz.
German (Töpel): habitational name from Töpeln near Leipzig or Töppeln near Gera, probably both deriving their names from Slavic topol ‘poplar’.
German: from Old Slavic toplъ ‘warm’, possibly applied as a nickname. Compare Toppel .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Childe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Germanic names formed with hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Hilda
meanings: Of Germanic origin, a Latinized short form of any of several girls' names derived from hild ‘battle’. Many of these are found in both Continental Germanic and Old English forms. St Hilda ( 614–80 ) was a Northumbrian princess who founded the abbey at Whitby and became its abbess. The name all but died out by the 14th century. It was strongly revived in the 19th century. Since the 1930s, however, it has again fallen from favour.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger Childe Baron de Torpel was born in 1055, in Le Puy, Creuse, Limousin, France, his father, Alwin Childegaire, was 30 and his mother, Lady Alwin Child, was 29. He married Baroness Roger de Torpel in 1089. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1130, in Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 75, and was buried in Peterborough, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger Childe Baron de Torpel est né en 1055, à Le Puy, Creuse, Limousin, France, son père, Alwin Childegaire, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady Alwin Child, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Baroness Roger de Torpel en 1089. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1130, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Peterborough, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger Childe Baron de Torpel 1055 in Le Puy, Creuse, Limousin, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alwin Childegaire 30 und seine Mutter Lady Alwin Child 29. Er hat 1089 Baroness Roger de Torpel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1130 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Northamptonshire, England und wurde in Peterborough, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Childe Baron de Torpel è nato il nel 1055 nel Le Puy, Creuse, Limousin, Francia, il suo padre, Alwin Childegaire, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Lady Alwin Child, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Baroness Roger de Torpel nel 1089. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1130, nel Northamptonshire, England, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolto a Peterborough, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger Childe Baron de Torpelが1055年にフランスリモウシンクレウゼル プウイで生まれた時，父Alwin Childegaireは30歳，母Lady Alwin Childは29歳であった。1089年にBaroness Roger de Torpelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1130年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで75歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアペテルボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Childe Baron de Torpel(이)가 1055년 프랑스 리모우신 크레우스 일 푸이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alwin Childegaire(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Alwin Child(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1089년 Baroness Roger de Torpel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1130년 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 피터버루그h에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Childe Baron de Torpel nasceu em 1055, em Le Puy, Creuse, Limousin, França, seu pai, Alwin Childegaire, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alwin Child, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Baroness Roger de Torpel em 1089. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1130, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 75 anos, e foi sepultado em Peterborough, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger Childe Baron de Torpel родился в 1055 г. в местности л пуи, креус, лимусин, Франция, его отцу по имени Alwin Childegaire было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Alwin Child, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Baroness Roger de Torpel в 1089 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1130 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности питирбороую, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger Childe Baron de Torpel nació en 1055, en Le Puy, Creuse, Limousin, Francia, su papá, Alwin Childegaire, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lady Alwin Child, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Baroness Roger de Torpel en 1089. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1130, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Peterborough, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
